H5301.strongs_def: to puff, in various applications (literally, to inflate, blow hard, scatter, kindle, expire; figuratively, to disesteem)
H5301.kjv_def: blow, breath, give up, cause to lose (life), seething, snuff.
H5301.usage: blow(4x), breathe(2x), seething(2x), blown(1x), lose(1x), snuffed(1x)

H5303.strongs_def: properly, a feller, i.e. a bully or tyrant

H5304.derivation: plural from an unused root meaning to scatter; expansions;

H5305.strongs_def: Naphish, a son of Ishmael, and his posterity

H5306.derivation: from an unused root meaning to glisten;
H5306.strongs_def: shining; a gem, probably the garnet

H5307.strongs_def: to fall, in a great variety of applications (intransitive or causative, literal or figurative)
H5307.kjv_def: be accepted, cast (down, self, (lots), out), cease, die, divide (by lot), (let) fail, (cause to, let, make, ready to) fall (away, down, -en, -ing), fell(-ing), fugitive, have (inheritance), inferior, be judged (by mistake for H6419 (פָּלַל)), lay (along), (cause to) lie down, light (down), be ([idiom] hast) lost, lying, overthrow, overwhelm, perish, present(-ed, -ing), (make to) rot, slay, smite out, [idiom] surely, throw down.
H5307.usage: fall(318x), cast(18x), divide(5x), overthrow(5x), present(5x), lay(3x), rot(3x), accepted(2x), inferior(2x), lighted(2x), lost(2x)

H5308.strongs_def: {to fall, in a great variety of applications (intransitive or causative, literal or figurative)}

H5310.strongs_def: to dash to pieces, or scatter
H5310.kjv_def: be beaten in sunder, break (in pieces), broken, dash (in pieces), cause to be discharged, dispersed, be overspread, scatter.
H5310.usage: scatter(3x), break(3x), dash(2x), discharged(1x), dispersed(1x), overspread(1x), sunder(1x)

H5312.strongs_def: to issue; causatively, to bring out

H5314.strongs_def: to breathe; passively, to be breathed upon , i.e. (figuratively) refreshed (as if by a current of air)

H5315.strongs_def: properly, a breathing creature, i.e. animal of (abstractly) vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense (bodily or mental)
H5315.kjv_def: any, appetite, beast, body, breath, creature, [idiom] dead(-ly), desire, [idiom] (dis-) contented, [idiom] fish, ghost, [phrase] greedy, he, heart(-y), (hath, [idiom] jeopardy of) life ([idiom] in jeopardy), lust, man, me, mind, mortally, one, own, person, pleasure, (her-, him-, my-, thy-) self, them (your) -selves, [phrase] slay, soul, [phrase] tablet, they, thing, ([idiom] she) will, [idiom] would have it.
H5315.usage: soul(475x), life(117x), person(29x), mind(15x), heart(15x), creature(9x), body(8x), himself(8x), yourselves(6x), dead(5x), will(4x), desire(4x), man(3x), themselves(3x), any(3x), appetite(2x)

H5317.derivation: from H5130 (נוּף) in the sense of shaking to pieces;
H5317.strongs_def: a dripping i.e. of honey (from the comb)

H5318.derivation: from H6605 (פָּתַח); opened, i.e. a spring;

H5319.strongs_def: properly, wrestled; but used (in the plural) transitively, a struggle

H5321.strongs_def: Naphtali, a son of Jacob, with the tribe descended from him, and its territory
H5321.pos: proper locative noun, proper masculine noun

H5322.strongs_def: a flower (from its brilliancy); also a hawk (from it flashing speed)

H5324.strongs_def: to station, in various applications (literally or figuratively)
H5324.kjv_def: appointed, deputy, erect, establish, [idiom] Huzzah (by mistake for a proper name), lay, officer, pillar, present, rear up, set (over, up), settle, sharpen, establish, (make to) stand(-ing, still, up, upright), best state.
H5324.usage: stand(34x), set(12x), officers(6x), upright(2x), appointed(1x), deputy(1x), erected(1x), establish(1x), Huzzab(1x)

H5326.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5324 (נָצַב);

H5327.strongs_def: properly, to go forth, i.e. (by implication) to be expelled, and (consequently) desolate; causatively, to lay waste; also (specifically), to quarrel

H5329.derivation: a primitive root;  also as denominative from H5331 (נֶצַח)
H5329.strongs_def: properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. to be eminent (as a superintendent, especially of the Temple services and its music); to be permanent
H5329.kjv_def: excel, chief musician (singer), oversee(-r), set forward.

H5331.strongs_def: properly, a goal, i.e. the bright object at adistance travelled towards; hence (figuratively), splendor, or (subjectively) truthfulness, or (objectively) confidence; but usually (adverbially), continually (i.e. to the most distant point of view)
H5331.kjv_def: alway(-s), constantly, end, ([phrase] n-) ever(more), perpetual, strength, victory.
H5331.usage: ever(24x), never(4x), perpetual(3x), always(2x), end(2x), victory(2x), strength(2x), alway(1x), constantly(1x), evermore(1x)

H5332.derivation: probably identical with H5331 (נֶצַח), through the idea of brilliancy of color;
H5332.strongs_def: juice of the grape (as blood red)

H5333.strongs_def: something stationary, i.e. a prefect, a military post, a statue

H5336.strongs_def: properly, conservative; but used passively, delivered

H5337.strongs_def: to snatch away, whether in a good or a bad sense
H5337.kjv_def: [idiom] at all, defend, deliver (self), escape, [idiom] without fail, part, pluck, preserve, recover, rescue, rid, save, spoil, strip, [idiom] surely, take (out).
H5337.usage: deliver(179x), recover(5x), rid(3x), escape(2x), rescue(2x), spoil(2x)

H5341.strongs_def: to guard, in a good sense (to protect, maintain, obey, etc.) or a bad one (to conceal, etc.)
H5341.kjv_def: besieged, hidden thing, keep(-er, -ing), monument, observe, preserve(-r), subtil, watcher(-man).
H5341.usage: keep(38x), preserve(13x), watchmen(3x), besieged(2x), keeper(1x), monuments(1x), preserver(1x), subtil(1x), watchers(1x)

H5342.derivation: from H5341 (נָצַר) in the sense of greenness as a striking color;

H5343.derivation: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H5352 (נָקָה);

H5344.strongs_def: to puncture, literally (to perforate, with more or less violence) or figuratively (to specify, designate, libel)
H5344.kjv_def: appoint, blaspheme, bore, curse, express, with holes, name, pierce, strike through.
H5344.usage: curse(6x), expressed(6x), blaspheme(3x), bore(2x), name(2x), pierce(2x), Appoint(1x), holes(1x)

H5348.derivation: from an unused root meaning to mark (by puncturing or branding);

H5349.derivation: active participle from the same as H5348 (נָקֹד);
H5349.strongs_def: a spotter (of sheep or cattle), i.e. the owner or tender (who thus marks them)

H5350.strongs_def: a crumb (as broken to spots); also a biscuit (as pricked)

H5352.strongs_def: to be (or make) clean (literally or figuratively); by implication (in an adverse sense) to be bare, i.e. extirpated
H5352.kjv_def: acquit [idiom] at all, [idiom] altogether, be blameless, cleanse, (be) clear(-ing), cut off, be desolate, be free, be (hold) guiltless, be (hold) innocent, [idiom] by no means, be quit, be (leave) unpunished, [idiom] utterly, [idiom] wholly.
H5352.usage: unpunished(11x), guiltless(5x), innocent(5x), clear(4x), cleanse(3x), free(2x), acquit(2x), altogether(2x), blameless(1x), desolate(1x), quit(1x), utterly(1x), wholly(1x)

H5353.derivation: feminine of H5348 (נָקֹד) (in the figurative sense of marked); distinction;

H5355.kjv_def: blameless, clean, clear, exempted, free, guiltless, innocent, quit.
H5355.usage: innocent(31x), guiltless(4x), quit(2x), blameless(2x), clean(1x), clear(1x), exempted(1x), free(1x), variant(1x)

H5357.derivation: from an unused root meaning to bore;

H5358.strongs_def: to grudge, i.e. avenge or punish
H5358.kjv_def: avenge(-r, self), punish, revenge (self), [idiom] surely, take vengeance.
H5358.usage: avenge(18x), vengeance(4x), revenge(4x), take(4x), avenger(2x), punished(2x), surely(1x)

H5360.strongs_def: avengement, whether the act of the passion

H5362.strongs_def: to strike with more or less violence (beat, fell, corrode); by implication (of attack) to knock together, i.e. surround or circulate
H5362.kjv_def: compass (about, -ing), cut down, destroy, go round (about), inclose, round.
H5362.usage: compass(7x), destroy(1x), down(1x), inclosed(1x), kill(1x), round(1x)

H5365.kjv_def: dig, pick out, pierce, put (thrust) out.

H5367.strongs_def: to entrap (with a noose), literally or figuratively

H5368.derivation: (Aramaic) corresponding to H5367 (נָקַשׁ); used in the sense of H5362 (נָקַף);

H5371.strongs_def: Nergal-Sharetser, the name of two Babylonians

H5372.derivation: from an unused root meaning to roll to pieces;

H5374.derivation: or נֵרִיָּהוּ; from H5216 (נִיר) and H3050 (יָהּ); light of Jah;

H5375.strongs_def: to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absolute and relative
H5375.kjv_def: accept, advance, arise, (able to, (armor), suffer to) bear(-er, up), bring (forth), burn, carry (away), cast, contain, desire, ease, exact, exalt (self), extol, fetch, forgive, furnish, further, give, go on, help, high, hold up, honorable ([phrase] man), lade, lay, lift (self) up, lofty, marry, magnify, [idiom] needs, obtain, pardon, raise (up), receive, regard, respect, set (up), spare, stir up, [phrase] swear, take (away, up), [idiom] utterly, wear, yield.
H5375.usage: bear(115x), take(58x), bare(34x), carry(30x), borne(22x), armourbearer(18x), forgive(16x), accept(12x), exalt(8x), regard(5x), obtained(4x), respect(3x)

H5377.strongs_def: to lead astray, i.e. (mentally) to delude, or (morally) to seduce

H5378.derivation: a primitive root (perhaps identical with H5377 (נָשָׁא), through the idea of imposition);
H5378.strongs_def: to lend on interest; by implication, to dun fordebt

H5380.strongs_def: to blow; by implication, to disperse

H5381.kjv_def: ability, be able, attain (unto), (be able to, can) get, lay at, put, reach, remove, wax rich, [idiom] surely, (over-) take (hold of, on, upon).
H5381.usage: overtake(23x), hold(5x), get(3x), attain(2x), obtain(2x), reach(2x), able(1x), bring(1x), layeth(1x), put(1x), remove(1x), surely(1x), take(1x)

H5382.strongs_def: to forget; figuratively, to neglect; causatively, to remit, remove

H5383.derivation: a primitive root (rather identical with H5382 (נָשָׁה), in the sense of H5378 (נָשָׁא));
H5383.strongs_def: to lend or (by reciprocity) borrow on security or interest
H5383.kjv_def: creditor, exact, extortioner, lend, usurer, lend on (taker on) usury.

H5384.derivation: from H5382 (נָשָׁה), in the sense of failure;
H5384.strongs_def: rheumatic or crippled (from the incident to Jacob)

H5385.derivation: or rather, נְשֻׂאָה; feminine. passive participle of H5375 (נָשָׂא);

H5387.strongs_def: properly, an exalted one, i.e. a king or sheik; also a rising mist
H5387.kjv_def: captain, chief, cloud, governor, prince, ruler, vapour.
H5387.usage: prince(96x), captain(12x), chief(10x), ruler(6x), vapours(3x), governor(1x), clouds(1x), part(1x)

H5391.strongs_def: to strike with a sting (as a serpent); figuratively, to oppress with interest on a loan

H5394.strongs_def: to pluck off, i.e. divest, eject or drop
H5394.kjv_def: cast (out), drive, loose, put off (out), slip.

H5395.strongs_def: properly, to blow away, i.e. destroy

H5397.strongs_def: a puff, i.e. wind, angry or vital breath, divine inspiration, intellect. or (concretely) an animal

H5398.strongs_def: to breeze, i.e. blow up fresh (as the wind)

H5399.strongs_def: properly, a breeze, i.e. (by implication) dusk (when the evening breeze prevails)
H5399.kjv_def: dark, dawning of the day (morning), night, twilight.
